name: Ida Hyde's Texas Sheet Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Nuts, chopped or to taste
recipeInstructions:
Cake: In a large mixing bowl, combine the sugar, flour, and baking soda. In a saucepan, melt the margarine; stir in cocoa powder, water, and bring to a boil. Pour the hot mixture over the dry ingredients and mix well with buttermilk, beaten eggs, and salt. Pour into a greased jelly roll pan. Bake at 400°F for about 15 minutes, until the top springs back when touched.
Frosting: In the same saucepan used for the cake, melt the margarine. Stir in cocoa powder, buttermilk, and bring to a rapid boil. Remove from heat, stir in confectioners' sugar and vanilla; blend thoroughly. Add nuts. Spread over the cake as soon as it comes out of the oven. Be prepared to clean up some drips.
If there are kids around, let them clean up the drips - this frosting is like fudge! The frosting is so good - it's almost like the cake exists just to give a base for the frosting. Mom cuts the frosting recipe down (about in half) but I prefer it just the way it is - lots.
